# Real-capture fixture provenance

Third-party Cisco / Aruba / FortiGate / OPNsense / MikroTik config snippets
used as **real-world parser validation fixtures**.  None of these files are
authored by netcanon; they are included verbatim under their upstream
licenses for the sole purpose of exercising our codec parsers against
configs the project didn't itself design.

This directory is intentionally *not* a `.gitignore`d downloads folder —
the fixtures are committed so CI can detect regressions against the exact
bytes we validated against, and so future contributors don't have to
re-discover them.

---

## cisco_iosxe/

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ntc_carrier_interfaces.txt` | [networktocode/ntc-templates](https://github.com/networktocode/ntc-templates) `tests/cisco_ios/show_running-config_interface/cisco_ios_show_running-config_interface.raw` | Apache-2.0 | Carrier-grade IOS interfaces with VRFs, sub-interfaces (dot1Q Q-in-Q), QoS service-policies, uRPF, ACL groups.  Stress-tests features our codec doesn't model. |
| `batfish_cisco_interface.txt` | [batfish/batfish](https://github.com/batfish/batfish) `tests/parsing-tests/networks/unit-tests/configs/cisco_interface` | Apache-2.0 | Grammar kitchen-sink — every interface sub-command Batfish's parser supports on one Ethernet.  "Parse doesn't crash" stress test. |
| `batfish_cisco_ip_route.txt` | [batfish/batfish](https://github.com/batfish/batfish) `tests/parsing-tests/networks/unit-tests/configs/cisco_ip_route` | Apache-2.0 | Static-route variants including `ip route ... name`, `track`, administrative distance, tag, `permanent`. |
| `batfish_cisco_aaa.txt` | [batfish/batfish](https://github.com/batfish/batfish) `tests/parsing-tests/networks/unit-tests/configs/cisco_aaa` | Apache-2.0 | AAA accounting/authentication/authorization stanzas — tests the parser's tolerance for commands it doesn't model yet. |
| `batfish_cisco_snmp.txt` | [batfish/batfish](https://github.com/batfish/batfish) `tests/parsing-tests/networks/unit-tests/configs/cisco_snmp` | Apache-2.0 | `snmp-server community`, `snmp-server group`, `snmp-server user`, and trap destinations.  Real community names / view definitions. |
| `batfish_cisco_logging.txt` | [batfish/batfish](https://github.com/batfish/batfish) `tests/parsing-tests/networks/unit-tests/configs/cisco_logging` | Apache-2.0 | `logging host`, `logging buffered`, `logging facility`, etc.  Not canonically modelled yet — serves as "parse doesn't crash" validation. |
| `racc_csr1_iosxe173_umbrella_sig.txt` | [nickrusso42518/racc](https://github.com/nickrusso42518/racc) `samples_nohash/csr1_20210629T142431/show_running-config.txt` | BSD-3-Clause | Real `show running-config` from a CSR1000v on **IOS-XE 17.3** — 398 lines.  Exercises EIGRP CITYNET, OSPF, 22 static routes (Cisco Umbrella SIG anycast targets + default via Tunnel100), IKEv2 + IPsec profiles, SIG tunnel interface with `tunnel protection ipsec profile`, SSH pubkey-chain + `key-hash`, `ip access-list standard`, guestshell app-hosting, NETCONF-YANG candidate-datastore, 2 local users (admin priv 15 + ec2-user priv 15), PKI self-signed + SLA trustpoints. |
| `racc_csr1000v_iosxe169_bgp_ospf.txt` | [nickrusso42518/racc](https://github.com/nickrusso42518/racc) `samples_hash/csr2_20230811T074823/show_running-config.txt` | BSD-3-Clause | Real `show running-config` from a CSR1000v on **IOS-XE 16.9** — 280 lines.  Exercises BGP AS 65001 (with vpnv4 + rtfilter address-families), OSPF process 1, 11 interfaces (8 Loopbacks + 3 GigabitEthernet), class-map + policy-map QoS policies applied per-interface, logging-host syslog targets, `ip access-list extended`, NETCONF-YANG + RESTCONF, 2 local users (`developer` + `root` priv 15), `enable secret`, MOTD banner.  16.9 LTS is the oldest well-supported LTS we wire through; canary for pre-17.x grammar quirks. |
| `racc_cat8000v_iosxe179_netconf.txt` | [nickrusso42518/racc](https://github.com/nickrusso42518/racc) `samples_hash/csr1_20230811T074823/show_running-config.txt` | BSD-3-Clause | Real `show running-config` from a Cat8000V on **IOS-XE 17.9** — 343 lines.  Exercises `ip nat inside source list ... overload` (PAT), `ip access-list extended NAT-ACL`, `telemetry ietf subscription` (YANG-push periodic update-policy over grpc-tcp), `app-hosting appid guestshell`, PKI self-signed + SLA trustpoints, RESTCONF + NETCONF-YANG, AAA local + SSH pubkey, `username X secret 9 $9$...` (type-9 hash).  Cat8000V is the C-line (Catalyst) virtual router — same codec, different platform image. |
| `user_contrib_cat9300_iosxe1712.txt` | User-contributed real `show running-config` from a physical **Cisco Catalyst 9300-24UX** running **IOS-XE 17.12**.  Captured via Netcanon's own backup layer against the contributor's live home-lab switch.  Sanitised: the two `username X secret 9 $9$...` lines were replaced with synthetic-marked `$9$fakeSalt...$fakeHash...ExampleValue...` placeholders (per AGENTS.md hard rule: never commit real hashes from non-public devices); the `Last configuration change ... by <windows-username>` stanza was updated to `by netadmin`.  All RFC1918 addressing, VLAN IDs (1/10/11/20/100/150), infrastructure-describing interface descriptions (`TRUNK - FORTIGATE`, `pvenas 2x 10gbe trunk`, `pveblade lacp`), and the self-signed device cert chain were retained for grammar coverage — same convention as `mikrotik/user_contrib_crs310_ros7.rsc`. | CC0-1.0 *(user contribution)* | **The real physical-switch fixture for IOS-XE**: 491 lines, `switch 1 provision c9300-24ux`, 47 interfaces spanning Gi/TenG/FortyG/TwentyFiveG/AppG + Port-channel1-3 + Vlan SVIs + Gi0/0 in `vrf Mgmt-vrf`, 3 LACP EtherChannels (one per trunk role — Fortigate uplink, NAS LAG, blade LAG), 6 VLANs in trunk-allowed lists, `spanning-tree mode rapid-pvst`, full Cat9k `class-map system-cpp-police-*` / `policy-map system-cpp-policy` CPP (control-plane policer) grammar, 28 × `privilege exec level 5 show X` delegation table, multiple `line vty` ranges (0-4, 5-29, 30-31).  Closes the physical-switch coverage gap that the 3 racc virtual-router captures did not. |
| `cml_saumur_iosxe1712_pvrstp.txt` | [CiscoDevNet/cml-community](https://github.com/CiscoDevNet/cml-community) `lab-topologies/ccna/Domain_2/2.5-interpret_stp/saumur_PVRSTP_solution.yaml` — embedded `configuration: \|` block for the `saumur` node | BSD-3-Clause | Real `show running-config` on **IOS-XE 17.12** from a CML virtual `ioll2-xe` image (IOU L2 IOS-XE — the virtual IOS-XE chassis Cisco ships with CML).  147 lines.  Interface notation is `Ethernet0/N` (IOL, not `Gi1/0/N` physical), but grammar is authentic IOS-XE 17.12: `spanning-tree mode rapid-pvst`, `spanning-tree extend system-id`, `spanning-tree pathcost method long`, `spanning-tree vlan 1-4094 priority 4096`, `spanning-tree link-type point-to-point`, `spanning-tree cost 2000000`, `vtp version 1`, `vlan internal allocation policy ascending`, self-signed PKI trustpoint.  Complements the physical Cat9300 capture with spanning-tree-cost / PVRST+ grammar the Cat9300 doesn't exercise. |
| `cml_basic_forwarding_iosv_r1_ospf.txt` | [CiscoDevNet/cml-community](https://github.com/CiscoDevNet/cml-community) `lab-topologies/basic-forwarding-behavior.yaml` — embedded `configuration: \|-` block for the `R1` IOSv router node | BSD-3-Clause | Real `show running-config` on **Cisco IOSv 15.x** (CML virtual IOSv image) — adds **OSPF single-area multi-feature grammar** the existing corpus doesn't exercise: `router ospf 1` + `router-id 1.1.1.1` + `passive-interface` + 5 `network <addr> <wildcard> area 0` statements, plus per-interface `ip ospf cost 100` / `ip ospf cost 110` metric tuning.  Also exercises `encapsulation dot1Q <vlan>` on two subinterfaces of a single physical (`GigabitEthernet0/1.100` + `.200`) and the full IOSv banner block (three banner types with `^C` delimiters) that's parse-tolerance-tested but silently dropped.  IOSv ≠ IOS-XE in formal branding but shares grammar; the codec handles both via shared `cisco_iosxe_cli` parse paths.  All IPs are lab RFC1918 (`192.168.100.0/24`, `192.168.200.0/24`, `10.12.12.0/24`..`10.14.14.0/24`). |

## opnsense/

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `opnsense_core_default.xml` | [opnsense/core](https://github.com/opnsense/core) `src/etc/config.xml.sample` | BSD-2-Clause | Upstream default `config.xml` template.  Includes system, users, groups, webgui, timeservers, bogons, firewall bits. |
| `opnsense_service_test_config.xml` | [opnsense/core](https://github.com/opnsense/core) `src/opnsense/service/tests/config/config.xml` | BSD-2-Clause | Service-layer test config with real interface zones (wan/lan), DHCP client settings, DHCPv6 prefix delegation, gateway tracking. |
| `opnsense_acl_test_config.xml` | [opnsense/core](https://github.com/opnsense/core) `src/opnsense/mvc/tests/app/models/OPNsense/ACL/AclConfig/config.xml` | BSD-2-Clause | ACL model test — 4 groups (admins + 3 test groups with distinct priv lists) + 5 users (root + 3 per-group testers + restricted admin).  Richest local_users surface in the corpus. |
| `user_contrib_supergate_opn25.xml` | User-contributed real `config.xml` from a deployed OPNsense instance ("supergate") captured via Netcanon's own backup layer (SSH + `cat /conf/config.xml`).  Sanitised: 2 bcrypt `<password>` hashes replaced with synthetic `$2y$11$fakeBcrypt...` markers; the api-user `<apikeys>` field (API key + SHA-512 crypt hash) and the QFeedsConnector `tip_...` apikey replaced with synthetic placeholders; both self-signed cert-chain `<prv>` private-key blobs stripped (public certs retained for grammar coverage); the user's real domain (`[redacted-domain]`) replaced with `example.test` across all 22 occurrences; 13 real MAC addresses sanitised OUI-preserving (last 3 octets zeroed and per-MAC sequential counter); the SSH-capture artifacts (`cat /conf/config.xml` prefix and trailing shell prompt) stripped.  All RFC1918 addressing, VLAN IDs (10/11/20/100/150), operational comments (MAC-reconciliation history, port-forward descriptions, DHCP reservation commentary), hostname (`supergate`), and interface descriptions retained per the MikroTik CRS310 / Cat 9300 user-contribution precedents. | CC0-1.0 *(user contribution)* | **The real deployed-OPNsense fixture**: 2,302-line `/conf/config.xml` — 8 interfaces (wan/lan/opt1-5/loopback with per-zone `<descr>` USERVLAN/MGMTVLAN/SERVERVLAN/CLUSTERVLAN/IOTVLAN), 5 VLANs under `<vlans><vlan>` block (tagged 10/11/20/100/150 with per-VLAN `<descr>`), 2 local users with bcrypt hashes, per-zone DHCP server config with extensive static MAC reservations (~20 per zone with operational commentary), Unbound DNS with local overrides, NTP, SNMP, IPsec, WireGuard, routing tables, self-signed CA + server cert chain.  Surfaced a real codec bug: the render path silently dropped `CanonicalVlan` entries because there was no `<vlans>` render block — fix + regression test landed in the same commit. |
| `opnsense_paramiko_shell_capture.xml` | Synthesised for regression testing — `opnsense_core_default.xml` body with a `cat /conf/config.xml\r\r\n` prefix prepended to reproduce the exact byte shape the pre-fix `ParamikoShellCollector` wrote to disk.  Derived from a BSD-2-Clause upstream fixture. | BSD-2-Clause *(derived)* | **Regression fixture for the paramiko-shell command-echo bug**: OPNsense backups captured via SSH raw PTY shell (strategy=paramiko_shell) landed on disk with a literal `cat /conf/config.xml\r\r\n` preamble before the `<?xml` prolog, breaking `ET.fromstring` in the migration codec with `syntax error: line 1, column 0`.  Detection still reported 98% confidence (probe uses tolerant substring match).  Fix landed in two layers: (1) collector strips the command echo in `_collect_output` before returning (prevents NEW bad backups); (2) codec tolerates leading noise before the prolog via `_trim_xml_prologue` (rescues legacy backups already on disk).  This fixture is part of (2)'s regression suite — the real-capture harness parses + round-trips it to prove the rescue works against the exact byte shape the user reported. |

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ntc_ip_address_export.rsc` | [networktocode/ntc-templates](https://github.com/networktocode/ntc-templates) `tests/mikrotik_routeros/ip_address_export_verbose/mikrotik_routeros_ip_address_export_verbose.raw` | Apache-2.0 | Real RouterOS 6.48.6 `/export verbose` snippet.  Exercises the `# ... by RouterOS` banner + `/ip address` section with quoted comments. |
| `routeros_diff_verbose_export.rsc` | [adamcharnock/routeros-diff](https://github.com/adamcharnock/routeros-diff) `tests/test_files/verbose_export.rsc` | MIT | Real RouterOS 6.48.1 `/export verbose` — 484 lines from an RB952Ui-5ac2nD home router ("Quinta Router").  Covers `/interface bridge`, `/interface vlan` (named `gn-mgmt`), `/ip address`, `/ip dhcp-server network` + `/ip pool`, `/snmp`, `/interface wireless`, `/queue tree`.  Different OS version (6.48.1 vs 6.48.6). |
| `taqavi_initial_provisioning.rsc` | [AmirArsalanTaqavi/Mikrotik-Config-Examples](https://github.com/AmirArsalanTaqavi/Mikrotik-Config-Examples) `initial-provisioning.rsc` | MIT | Real MikroTik provisioning script (not a `/export` capture — a script an admin runs to provision an L009UiGS-2HaxD router).  Covers `/interface bridge` + `/interface bridge port`, `/interface ethernet` with per-port comments, `/ip service` restrictions, `/user add`, DHCP, WireGuard, firewall.  Password field contains the placeholder `ChangeMe123!` (called out in the source's own comment) — no real credential. |
| `user_contrib_crs310_ros7.rsc` | User-contributed real `/export verbose` from a CRS310-8G+2S+ running RouterOS 7.18.2.  Sanitised by `scripts/` (serial + software-id + MAC addresses replaced with non-identifying placeholders; infrastructure-describing interface names and RFC1918 IPs retained for grammar coverage). | CC0-1.0 *(user contribution)* | Real home-lab switch backing a Proxmox cluster + NAS.  630 lines covering a renamed ethernet port fleet (Desktop, Access Point, CLUSTER - PVE3/5/NAS, PROD - PVE3/5/NAS, UPLINKSFP), 5 VLANs (cluster/IOT/mgmt/server/user), bridge + bridge port, BGP template stub, IPv6 ND, l2tp-server, sstp-server, MPLS settings, system clock, system leds, system watchdog, tools, and a trove of other RouterOS 7 sections.  Third OS version for MikroTik (6.48.1 / 6.48.6 / 7.18.2) — the fixture that crossed MikroTik's `certified` threshold. |

## fortigate/

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `kevinguenay_fgt_70g_branch.conf` | [KevinGuenay/fortinet-resources](https://github.com/KevinGuenay/fortinet-resources) `blog_resources/fortigate_ztp/fortigate_configurations/FGT-70G-BRANCH.conf` | MIT | Real FortiOS 7.6.6 branch config for a FortiGate 70G ZTP deployment.  12,317 lines covering system global, interfaces (including `fortilink` + `LAG_INTERNAL` aggregates), VLAN subinterfaces (named `VL_100` / `VL_101`), BGP loopback, SD-WAN, IPsec, firewall policies, VIPs, web filtering, antivirus, IPS.  Our codec extracts the subset it models; the rest is silently carried past. |
| `kevinguenay_fgt_vm_hub.conf` | [KevinGuenay/fortinet-resources](https://github.com/KevinGuenay/fortinet-resources) `blog_resources/fortigate_ztp/fortigate_configurations/FGT-VM-HUB.conf` | MIT | Real FortiOS 7.6.6 VM-based hub config — 13,827 lines.  Hub-side counterpart to the branch config above.  Same OS version (gap to `certified` tier — need a fixture from a different OS version). |
| `user_contrib_fg100e_fos7213.conf` | User-contributed real `show full-configuration` from a physical **FortiGate 100E** running **FortiOS 7.2.13** (build 1762).  Captured via Netcanon's own backup layer.  Sanitised: 48 `ENC <base64>` encrypted secrets replaced with synthetic `ENC fakeEncodedSecret...` markers; 14 `-----BEGIN (ENCRYPTED) PRIVATE KEY-----` / `-----END` blocks stripped with `REMOVED_FIXTURE_SANITISATION` placeholder (public certificate chains retained for grammar coverage); 5 SSH public keys replaced with `AAAAfakeSSHPublicKey...` markers; 1 Firebase registration ID sanitised; real WAN IP `[redacted-WAN-IP]` replaced with RFC 5737 TEST-NET-3 `203.0.113.217` (6 occurrences in address objects + VIP extips); real email `[redacted-email]` replaced with `netadmin@example.test` (2 occurrences in admin contact fields).  All other real data retained — RFC1918 addressing, VLAN IDs, hostname `fortihome`, alias `FortiGate-100E`, interface descriptions, firewall policy rules with internal IPs, DHCP reservations, SD-WAN SLAs, SSL-VPN config. | CC0-1.0 *(user contribution)* | **The real physical-FG100E fixture**: 35K+ lines — 34 interfaces (physical WAN1/WAN2/internal1-N + VLANs VL_10/20/100/150/LAN_TRUNK + SSL-VPN tunnel), 5 VLAN subinterfaces, 2 LAGs, 6 DHCP servers, 3 admins, 1 RADIUS server (`Test` pointing at 192.168.20.20), 1 SNMP community, full firewall policy table with tens of policies, VIPs, SDWAN health-check config, IPsec, SSL-VPN portal, FortiGuard categories, web-filter rules.  First physical-appliance FortiGate capture in the corpus (KevinGuenay pair were VM + 70G branch); first FortiOS 7.2.x capture (others were 7.6.6). |

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aruba_central_5memberstack_rendered.cfg` | [aruba/central-sample-bulk-configurations](https://github.com/aruba/central-sample-bulk-configurations) `ArubaOS-Switch Templates/5MemberStack - Template/5memberStack - Template.txt` | BSD-2-Clause *(upstream)* | **Rendered** from Aruba Central's bulk-config template via `scripts/render_aruba_central_template.py` (defensible-defaults substitution + top-level-keyword dedent post-pass).  Real AOS-S grammar, but with placeholder values — see `aruba_aoss/README.md` for the "do better later" note. |
| `hpe_community_2930f_wc1607_intervlan.cfg` | [HPE Community forum thread 7026923](https://community.hpe.com/t5/aruba-provision-based/routing-not-working-as-expected-on-2930f-48g/td-p/7026923) | Forum share (user paste for troubleshooting) | Real 2930F `show running-config` on **WC.16.07.0002**.  12 VLANs with inter-VLAN L3, `ip helper-address` DHCP relay on 10 VLANs, `ip forward-protocol udp X dns` / `ntp`, 4 static routes (incl. `ip default-gateway` + `ip route 0.0.0.0/0`), `ip dns server-address`, `primary-vlan`, `timesync ntp`, `time daylight-time-rule`.  Email contact sanitised to `netadmin@example.test`; all addresses RFC1918. |
| `hpe_community_2920_wb1608_dhcp_snooping.cfg` | [HPE Community forum thread 7051607](https://community.hpe.com/t5/hpe-aruba-networking-provision/a-lot-of-packet-loss-in-switching-infrastructure/td-p/7051607) | Forum share (user paste for troubleshooting) | Real 2920 `show running-config` on **WB.16.08.0001** — different OS family (WB vs WC) and major version from the other captures.  Exercises `dhcp-snooping` with 13 `authorized-server` entries + VLAN scope + trust-port list, `ntp unicast` with `iburst` on peer, `ntp server <public>`, `web-management ssl`, `ip authorized-managers` ACLs, `snmp-server host ... trap-level critical`.  SNMP community already obscured `xxxx` by poster; all IPs RFC1918 except `194.25.134.196` (PTB public NTP, kept as real grammar). |
| `hpe_community_2930f_wc1610_dhcp_server.cfg` | [HPE Community forum thread 7084768](https://community.hpe.com/t5/aruba-provision-based/2930f-dhcp-server-vlan-setup/td-p/7084768) | Forum share (user paste for troubleshooting) | Real 2930F `show running-config` on **WC.16.10.0005**.  `dhcp-server pool` grammar exercised with 3 pools (default-router + dns-server + network + range per pool), 4 VLANs with `dhcp-server` enable flag, `allow-unsupported-transceiver`.  All IPs RFC1918 except public DNS `62.179.104.196, 213.46.228.196` (Ziggo Belgium ISP resolvers). |
| `user_contrib_2930m_wc1611.cfg` | User-contributed (direct paste, operator-provided) | Operator contribution for LTS-branch validation | Real 2930M stack `show running-config` on **WC.16.11.0025** — the most-current WC LTS branch and the first fixture covering 16.11.x.  Stacked switch family: `JL323A` chassis + `JL083A` flexible-module uplinks, stack-aware port ids (`1/1-1/47`, `1/A1-1/A4`).  Exercises `stacking` stanza, `oobm` per-member IP config, `include-credentials` + `password manager sha1` hash, SNMPv3 engineid, comma-separated VLAN port lists mixing slot-port + slot-letter-port ranges.  Password hash **sanitised** (`8bcbe690…0f8478` → synthetic `deadbeef0000000000000000000000000000dead`); MAC addresses + engine IDs retained as public hardware identifiers.  First-contact surfaced + fixed two latent codec bugs (port-range expansion on slot-port forms + LAG-member linking when `trunk` appears before `interface` stanzas in the rendered output) — see RESULTS.md findings. |
| `hpe_community_5406rzl2_kb1515.cfg` | [HPE Community forum thread 6935784](https://community.hpe.com/t5/Aruba-ProVision-based/DHCP-SERVER-and-multi-vlans/td-p/6935784) (posted by user `moise` while troubleshooting multi-VLAN DHCP helper-address behaviour) | Forum share (user paste for troubleshooting) | Real 5406Rzl2 (J9850A) modular-chassis `Configuration Editor` export on **KB.15.15.0008** — first fixture on the **KB software branch** (distinct from WC/WB already covered) and first fixture on the **modular-chassis class** (5400R series, as opposed to the 2920/2930F/2930M desktop/stackable boxes).  Exercises `module A type j9534a` + `module B type j9537a` line-card declarations, letter-slot-port ranges (`A1-A24`, `B1-B24`, comma-joined forms like `A2-A6,A8-A10,A12-A24`), `oobm` with `no ip address`, `ip helper-address` on multiple VLANs, inter-VLAN L3 with `ip routing`, `no autorun`, `no dhcp config-file-update`, `no dhcp image-file-update`.  All IPs RFC1918; hostname `HP-5406Rzl2` is clearly illustrative; MAC identifier string `05:18.7f.ff.3f.ef:4d` + firmware version retained as public hardware identifiers. |

## junos/

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `buraglio_netlab_junos184.set` | [buraglio/Juniper-SR-PCE](https://github.com/buraglio/Juniper-SR-PCE) `netlab-ns-junos-set` — netlab-ns lab config by Nick Buraglio (public SR-PCE demo) | no declared license, but content is a short `show configuration \| display set` output format matching a public ES.net netlab-ns demo — factual device-output provenance comparable to the HPE Community forum-share precedent.  If the author objects, drop the fixture. | Real **Junos 18.4R1-S1.1** set-form config from an ES.net netlab-ns demo.  28 lines.  First Junos fixture in the corpus.  Exercises `set version`, `set system host-name` FQDN form, `set system root-authentication encrypted-password` (parse-and-ignore: root-auth is distinct from user accounts), `set interfaces em0/lo0 unit 0 family inet address` CIDR form, `set interfaces em0 unit 0 family iso` (ISO-family / IS-IS) and `family inet6` / `family mpls` (parse-and-ignore: non-v4 families not canonically modelled in v1), `set routing-options autonomous-system`, `set protocols bgp`/`isis`/`mpls`/`lldp`/`topology-export` (all parse-and-ignore).  Hashed root password sanitisation preserved from upstream (already a demo-grade hash).  All IPs within 172.22.0.0/16 + 100.64.0.0/10 (RFC1918 / CGNAT space). |
| `ksator_labmgmt_qfx5100_junos173.set` | [ksator/lab_management](https://github.com/ksator/lab_management) `backup/QFX5100-183_config.2017-12-19@15:28:16` @ commit `37e42eb` | MIT — Copyright Juniper Networks 2018 | Real **Junos 17.3R1.10** set-form config from a QFX5100 data-centre access/leaf switch.  106 lines, pure set-form (0 non-set lines).  Second Junos fixture; distinct OS major (17.3 vs 18.4) and device class (QFX5100 DC vs vMX).  Exercises `ae0`/`ae1` aggregated-ethernet LAGs, 2× `et-0/0/48-49` 40G uplinks, 16 `set vlans V<N>` declarations, `apply-groups POC_Lab` inheritance pattern (host-name lives inside `set groups POC_Lab system host-name` — Tier-3 parse-and-ignore until v2b), `set system root-authentication encrypted-password`, RADIUS + SSH accept-data.  Surfaced the bare-interface round-trip fix on render — interfaces defined only through `unit 0 family ethernet-switching ...` (Tier-3) had no canonical attributes and were dropped on render before the placeholder-line fix. |
| `ksator_labmgmt_ex4550_junos151.set` | [ksator/lab_management](https://github.com/ksator/lab_management) `backup/EX4550-190_config.2017-12-19@15:28:15` @ commit `37e42eb` | MIT — Copyright Juniper Networks 2018 | Real **Junos 15.1R6.7** set-form config from an EX4550 campus/DC access switch.  52 lines, pure set-form.  Third Junos fixture; oldest OS major in Junos corpus (15.1 predates the 17.3 + 18.4 we already had).  Exercises `xe-0/0/0-2` 10G trunk ports with `unit 0 family ethernet-switching port-mode trunk` + `vlan members V<N>`, 6 VLAN declarations, `chassis aggregated-devices ethernet device-count 2`, `apply-groups POC_Lab` pattern.  Light grammar — the value is the third distinct Junos version + second `apply-groups`-style fixture in the corpus. |
| `ksator_labmgmt_qfx5110_junos173.set` | [ksator/lab_management](https://github.com/ksator/lab_management) `backup/QFX5110-169_config.2017-12-19@15:28:19` @ commit `37e42eb` | MIT — Copyright Juniper Networks 2018 | Real **Junos 17.3R1-S2.1** set-form config from a **QFX5110 48x10G+6x40G leaf switch** (newer ASIC generation than the existing QFX5100).  52 lines.  Adds a second leaf platform class to the corpus — QFX5110 differs from QFX5100 in interface speed mix (`et-0/0/48-53` 40G uplinks vs 2x40G on QFX5100) and supports VXLAN routing in hardware that QFX5100 doesn't.  Light grammar (basic apply-groups + `lo0`); the value is platform-class coverage rather than grammar density. |

> **Codec finding from fixture research:** A QFX10K2 spine capture
> from the same ksator/lab_management batch (`QFX10K2-174_config`)
> was investigated but surfaced a round-trip stability bug in the
> junos codec around EVPN ESI grammar — `set interfaces ae1 esi
> 00:55:55:55:55:55:55:55:55:55` + `set interfaces ae1 esi
> all-active` parses on the first pass but is not preserved through
> render → re-parse.  Fixture is held out of the corpus pending a
> codec fix; tracked as a separate work item.  Documented here so
> the next person who looks at ksator's QFX10K2 captures knows the
> fixture is intentionally absent rather than overlooked.

| `batfish_evpntype5_router1_junos2541.set` | [batfish/lab-validation](https://github.com/batfish/lab-validation) `snapshots/junos_evpn_type5/configs/router1/show_configuration_\|_display_set.txt` @ commit `d40faf6` | Apache-2.0 | Real **Junos 25.4R1.12** set-form config from Batfish's Junos EVPN Type-5 lab validation snapshot.  151 lines.  **Fourth Junos fixture covering a fourth distinct major** (15.1 + 17.3 + 18.4 + 25.4) — 25.4 is the jump to a current LTS that the ksator/buraglio corpus didn't cover.  Exercises the grammar surface GAP 6 will target: `set vlans <name> vxlan vni <vni>` mappings, 4 IRB sub-interfaces with per-unit `family inet address` + VRRP, `set routing-instances <name> instance-type vrf` + `route-distinguisher` + `vrf-target` + `interface`, `set protocols evpn encapsulation vxlan`, `set protocols bgp group EVPN_OVERLAY family evpn signaling`, `set policy-options policy-statement`.  EVPN Type-5 IP-prefix advertisements currently parse-and-ignore (matches the `/evpn-type5-routes/route` Unsupported declaration pending GAP 6 codec wire-up). |
| `batfish_l3vpn_pe1_junos2541.set` | [batfish/lab-validation](https://github.com/batfish/lab-validation) `snapshots/junos_l3vpn/configs/pe1/show_configuration_\|_display_set.txt` @ commit `d40faf6` | Apache-2.0 | Real **Junos 25.4R1.12** set-form config from Batfish's Junos L3VPN lab validation snapshot.  34 lines.  Fifth Junos fixture; same Junos 25.4R1.12 major as the EVPN fixture above but a distinct semantic surface: MPLS L3VPN provider-edge configuration with `CUSTOMERS` VRF (`set routing-instances CUSTOMERS instance-type vrf` + `interface` + `route-distinguisher 1.1.1.1:65001` + `vrf-target target:65001:1`), iBGP PE mesh with `family inet-vpn unicast`, LDP + MPLS on the core link, single static route.  The simpler, canonical L3VPN / MP-BGP VPNv4 grammar — complements the EVPN fixture by exercising the classic (pre-EVPN) VRF-over-MPLS path that enterprise edge routers use today.  Together the two 25.4 fixtures give us both VXLAN-EVPN and MPLS-L3VPN coverage for GAP 5's CanonicalRoutingInstance schema when it gets wired up. |

## arista_eos/

| File | Origin | License | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ksator_dcs_7150s64_eos4224.txt` | [ksator/arista_eos_audit](https://github.com/ksator/arista_eos_audit) `output/10.83.28.122/eos_commands/text/show running-config.txt` — part of a public audit-tool demo repo by ex-Arista engineer Khelil Sator | upstream repo is unlicensed, but author is a public Arista community contributor with many other permissively-licensed EOS projects (arista-netdevops-community / `pyeapi` contributor); content is `show running-config` output treated as factual device state, not creative authorship — comparable provenance class to the HPE Community forum-share precedent.  If the author objects, drop the fixture. | Real **DCS-7150S-64-CL** (64-port 10GbE data-center switch) `show running-config` on **EOS 4.22.4M-2GB**.  256 lines.  First Arista fixture in the corpus.  Exercises hostname + DNS + NTP + SNMP community + 5 local users (mix of `nopassword` / `secret sha512 $6$` / `secret 5 $1$`) + `spanning-tree mode mstp` + `aaa` blocks + 52 × `interface Ethernet<N>` + 16 × QSFP-breakout `interface Ethernet<N>/<M>` + `Loopback0` + `Management1` + single IP static route + parse-and-ignore `router bgp` / `management api http-commands` / `daemon TerminAttr` / `transceiver qsfp default-mode 4x10G`.  IP addresses look like real RFC1918 (`10.83.28.0/22` management + `10.10.10.0/31` p2p) but are public via upstream repo publication. |
| `batfish_labval_dc1_leaf2a_eos4230.txt` | [batfish/lab-validation](https://github.com/batfish/lab-validation) `snapshots/eos_evpn_l3_design_guide/configs/DC1-LEAF2A/show_running-config.txt` @ commit `d40faf61c940da7e225c63bb66da9e2487a1ccb8` | Apache-2.0 | Real **DC1-LEAF2A** (vEOS EVPN leaf from the Arista EVPN L3 Design Guide lab validation) `show running-config` on **EOS-4.23.0.1F**.  429 lines.  Second Arista fixture — different EOS major (4.22 vs 4.23).  DC-fabric kitchen-sink: MLAG peer link + MLAG across 5 Port-Channels (channel-groups 3/6/7/10/11), VXLAN1 overlay, 15 tenant VLANs (zones 110-4094), router bgp 65102, VRF definitions (`Tenant_A_OP_Zone`/`WEB_Zone`), interface Vlan3009 with VARP virtual-router MAC, 40 interfaces (Ethernet + Port-Channel).  Surfaced a latent LAG render bug: the render loop emitted `interface Port-ChannelN` stubs but not the matching `channel-group N mode <mode>` on member Ethernet interfaces — round-trip dropped all LAGs.  Fix + regression test (`test_render_lag_member_emits_channel_group` + `test_render_lag_member_mode_normalised`) landed in the same commit. |
| `batfish_duplicateprivate_eos4211.txt` | [batfish/lab-validation](https://github.com/batfish/lab-validation) `snapshots/eos_private_asn/configs/DuplicatePrivate/show_running-config.txt` @ commit `d40faf6` | Apache-2.0 | Real **DuplicatePrivate** (vEOS BGP-focused fixture from Batfish's Arista private-ASN snapshot) `show running-config` on **EOS-4.21.1.1F**.  64 lines.  Third Arista fixture — third distinct EOS major (4.21 + 4.22 + 4.23) closing the `certified` ≥3-fixture-from-≥2-version bar.  Light grammar: hostname, 1× `username admin secret sha512 $6$...`, 1× routed `interface Ethernet1` with `/24`, Ethernet2-16 + Loopback123 stubs, `router bgp 65001` with `neighbor ... remote-as 65001` (duplicate-private-ASN scenario).  EOS 4.21 is older than the 4.25+/4.27+/4.30+ ideal but still a distinct 3rd major; a newer LTS fixture remains a nice-to-have for `certified` quality once sourced. |
| `karneliuk_a_eos1_eos4260.txt` | [karneliuk-com/batfish-mvp](https://github.com/karneliuk-com/batfish-mvp) `snapshots/nat/configs/EOS1.cfg` @ commit `62e8ce7` | BSD-3-Clause — Copyright 2021 karneliuk.com | Real **A-EOS1** vEOS fixture from Anton Karneliuk's Batfish MVP demo — full EVPN/VXLAN kitchen-sink.  82 lines + `end`.  Banner: `! device: A-EOS1 (vEOS, EOS-4.26.0.1F)`.  **Fourth Arista fixture spanning a 4th distinct EOS major** (4.21 + 4.22 + 4.23 + 4.26).  Grammar surface: `service routing protocols model multi-agent`, `spanning-tree mode mstp`, local AAA with sha512 hash, VLAN 100 (Tenant_100), 2× Ethernet routed + Loopback0 + Management1 (IPv4+IPv6), **Vxlan1 with vni 100**, ip/ipv6 routing, ip prefix-list, static default route, route-maps (permit+deny), **router bgp 65033** with two eBGP neighbors, `router bgp / vlan RD+RT / evpn redistribute-learned`, `address-family evpn` + `address-family ipv4` with route-map in/out filter.  Promotes the Arista corpus beyond the 4.21-4.23 range that predated the GAP 6 EVPN/VXLAN codec wire-up. |

---

## Adding new captures

1. Fetch from an unambiguously-licensed public source (Apache, MIT, BSD).
2. Drop into `<vendor>/` with a filename that encodes origin + feature.
3. Update this NOTICE with an entry covering origin URL, license, and
   what the file stresses.
4. The parametrized harness at `tests/unit/migration/test_real_captures.py`
   picks up every `*.txt` / `*.cfg` / `*.xml` / `*.conf` / `*.rsc` /
   `*.set` under `<vendor>/` automatically — no further wiring needed.
